summary:
Perfect! 🎯 

**The needle found!**

The off-by-one array slice bug was in `/app/processor.py` at line 15:
- **Bug**: `end = start + batch_size - 1`
- **Fix**: `end = start + batch_size`

The issue was that Python slicing uses an exclusive upper bound, so `records[start:end-1]` drops the last item of each batch. This caused records to be lost when batch processing, especially noticeable at batch boundaries.

The test now passes all 4 test cases:
1. ✅ Processing 25 records in batches of 10 returns all 25
2. ✅ Processing 10 records in batches of 10 (exact boundary) returns all 10
3. ✅ Processing 7 records in batches of 3 (uneven division) returns all 7
4. ✅ All record IDs are present with no drops
